project.project_name: Incheon Offshore Wind Farm Geophysical Survey

comments.section: Table 2. Survey Equipment Setup, p.11 of 85
comments.comment_text: It's undestood that Sunyoung No.2 will not use USBL due to shallow water in her survey area and a laid-back solution will be used. Can Geoview add a vessel column in Table 2, to specify the equipment onboard each survey vessel (Geoview DP-1 & Sunyoung No.2)?
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP Table 2.

comments.section: Table 3. Scope of Work (Geophysical Survey), p.11 of 85
comments.comment_text: In Table 3, there is UHRS scope (5.62km, lines for RCS locations) being put in Lot 3, note that UHRS scope will only be included in Lot 1 scope (the scope to be based on equipment/sensor spread, not location - to avoid commercial confusion since UHRS price items only present in Lot 1). I assume Table 3 refes to Geoview DP-1's Lot 1 & Lot 3 scopes, please amend the table tile (like the Table 4 title) to make it clear the scopes plan to be carried out be Geoview DP-1.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP Table 3.

comments.section: Table 3. Scope of Work (Geophysical Survey), p.11 of 85
comments.comment_text: RCS line plan (additional lines) have been updated when the PEP R2 was prepared, just to clarify that we will use the latest line plan as the planned scope (> 5.62 km). Will be appreciated if this can be updated in next PEP revision.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP Table 3.

comments.comment_text: Please use clear table to show the schedule, not screengrab of the gantt chart (cannot see clearly the dates/duration when zooming in).
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP Table 6.

comments.comment_text:
Please update a realistic schedule for Sunyoung No.2, mob start 05-Mar-25 not realistic now. Note that below items should be closed/done before we can officially start the mobilisation for Sunyoung No.2:
i) all the documentation (PEP and HSE docs) to closed/accepted
ii) a full HIRA to be gone through with Geoview/Sunyoung/Orsted
iii) VKOM & 2nd HIRA (ideally to be arranged on mob start date)
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP Table 6.

comments.section: 1.6. Plans for Port Calls, p.13 of 85
comments.comment_text:
'The primary port for Lot 1 will be Pyeongtaek Port, with Incheon North Port as a backup if the primary port is unavailable. The primary port for Lot 3 will be Incheon North Port, with Pyeongtaek Port as a backup.'
- To clarify portcall plan will be more related to the 24hr vessel Geoview DP-1, which assums to have 28-30 days offshore endurance and she should make best use of the weather down time for potential need on crew change, food supply, bunkering, etc.
- Any different port plans or port limitations for 2 different vessels (Geoview DP-1 & Sunyoung No.2)? Reasons why Pyeongtaek Port as main port for Lot 1 and Incheon North Port as primary port for Lot 3? What's the transit time from Pyeongtaek Port and Incheon North Port to our project site respectively?
comments.response_text:
Noted.
- Pyeongtaek Port has been designated as the alternative port, while Incheon North Port will serve as the primary port for both Lot 1 and Lot 3 due to its close proximity to each survey area. Below is a summary of the distance and transit time between each survey area and its respective primary and alternative port:
* Lot 1 to Incheon North Port: 95.33 km, approximately 7 hours 30 minutes
* Lot 1 to Pyeongtaek Port: 110.33 km, approximately 8 hours 40 minutes
* Lot 3 to Incheon North Port: 28.46 km, approximately 2 hours 10 minutes
* Lot 3 to Pyeongtaek Port: 58.86 km, approximately 4 hours 40 minutes

comments.section: 1.6. Plans for Port Calls, p.13 of 85
comments.comment_text:
Thanks for the useful informaiton on the main/back-up port, my further questions:
- For the main or back-up port, will there be any port limitations for our offshore and nearshore vessels (e.g. vessel type, vessel draft, tide, etc.)?
- It's mentioned Incheon North Port will be used as primary port for Lot 3, I assume DP-1 will use Incheon New Port for her Lot 1 + Lot 2 + Lot 3 scopes and Sunyoung No.2 will use Incheon New Port for her Lot 2/ Lot 3 scope, and Pyeongtaek Port will be alternative/ back-up port for both vessels. Can you clarify if this is correct understanding? If so, will be appreciated if you can update the outdated info put in the PEP.
comments.response_text: DP-1 will use Incheon North Port for Lot 1, Lot 2, and Lot 3, with Pyeongtaek Port as the alternative port. Sunyoung No.2 will enter and depart from Incheon Namhang Coastal Pier (Hangdong 1-ga, Jung-gu, Incheon) for Lot 2 and Lot 3 scopes. For long-term berthing, it will dock at Namhang Excursion Boat Terminal. If berthing at these two ports is not possible, Samgilpo Port in Seosan will be used as an alternative. This information has been updated in the PEP Chapter 1.6

comments.section: Table 6. Personnel Information & Table 7. SUNYOUNG Personnel Information, p.15 of 85
comments.comment_text:
- Assuming some people in Table 6 are the project members to manage the overall project, including both Geoview DP-1 and Sunyoung No.2 scopes. Can you make clear table(s) to show who will play the roles responsible for the overall project (e.g. PM, HSE Manager, etc.) and who will have duty on specific vessel only (e.g. party chief, surveyors, QC engineer, marine crew, onboard HSE, etc.)?
- For Sunyoung No.2 personnel (Table 7), can you also add description on responsibility of each position listed?
To include but not limited to:
i) who will be the party chief/ pm onboard (Sunyoung or Geoview personnel?), i.e. who will manage the overall operation for Sunyoung No.2 (look after/ plan/ adjust the survey operation and to align with Geoview PM/ Jongnam when needed)?
ii) who will be the licensed specialist who will report to MOF, under MSRA permit requirement?
iii) any dedicated HSE personnel will be onboard?
iv) technical personnel, who wiil check the data onboard?
v) etc.
- 'The personnel from GEOVIEW assigned to support the mobilization and calibration of SUNYOUNG No.2 are listed in Table 7; however, the assigned personnel may be subject to change depending on the schedule.', we'll hope Geoview can fix the Sunyoung No.2 POB list soon, also be noted that the personnel need to upload required safety training/medical certificates and get approved on SOMS before they can sail out.
comments.response_text:
It has been updated in PEP section 2.
i) The Party Chief, Dongwan Lee, will be a Geoview personnel and will also be responsible for onboard HSE management.
ii). The mid-level technical personnel onboard Geoview DP-1 will report to MOF under the MSRA permit requirements.
iii) No dedicated HSE personnel will be assigned onboard.
iv) There will be no separate QC personnel. One of the surveyors will be responsible for real-time data monitoring and acquisition during the survey.

comments.section: Table 6. Personnel Information & Table 7. SUNYOUNG Personnel Information, p.15 of 85
comments.comment_text:
i) Will the Party Chief (Dongwan Lee) look after/manage the overall operation for Sunyoung No.2, including Subcontractor/Sunyoung's scope (MBES)? Will the Party Chief need to report to/discuss with the PM (Jongnam Yoon) for aligning the Sunyoung No.2's survey plan/adjustment?
ii) We understand from Geoview (Kevin Lee) that there might only be one licensed speciliast available for our 2 survey vessels running in parallel. Our Consent colleague have helped to clarify with MOF that under this circumstance, MOF agree to have only one licensed specialist to report 2 vessels operation details to them.
iv) Who will be the surveyor responsible for real-time data monitoring (data quality check), Geoview or Sunyoung personnel? Will the Party Chief/Surveyor be able to communicate with our Offshore Supervisor (OS) to check the survey plan/data quality (no language barrier, Ortsed OS can only speak English)?
comments.response_text:
i) The Party Chief, Dongwan Lee, will be responsible for calibration and will oversee the overall operation of the Sunyoung No.2 survey under the approval of the PM, Jongnam Yoon.

ii) Noted.

iii) The surveyor acquiring the data will monitor it in real-time. MBES will be handled by Kyunghoon Kim from SUNYOUNG, and SBP will be managed by Minjoong Kim from GEOVIEW. QC will be conducted after the data is transferred to the office following a 12-hour survey period. The Party Chief is not fluent in English.

comments.comment_text: 24hr operation will only apply for Geoview DP-1, please differentiate the plan (Figure 4) for the 12hr operation vessel Sunyoung No.2. What's the shift/rotation plan for Sunyoung No.2?
comments.response_text:
It has been updated in PEP section 2.2.
SUNYOUNG No.2 will operate on a 12-hour daytime shift with full-time work during the shift. Breaks will be assigned for a certain duration every hour.

comments.section: Figure 5. Organization and Communication Flow Chart, p.17 of 85
comments.comment_text:
Please provide more details and specify all responsibility split between Geoview amd Sunyoung (subcontractor), including but not limited to:
- Survey equipment installation: will Sunyoung be responsible for MBES installtion only? What about SBP and SSS/MAG?
- Calibration of equipment: will Sunyoung be responsible for MBES calibration only? What about SBP and SSS/MAG? Geoview should make sure all equipment calibrations be able to meet Orsted requirements.
- Operating of Equipment: will Sunyoung be responsible for operating MBES only? What about SBP and SSS/MAG? Will Geoview take lead/manage overall survey operation, including MBES?
- What about the during operation data QA/QC and who will be doing the following data processing and interpretation?
- Who will be reporting to MHCC for vessel movements/ emergencies?
- Any 'On-site HSE Manager' will be onboard Sunyoung No.2?
- Etc.
comments.response_text:
Sunyoung will be responsible for the installation and calibration of the MBES system, while Geoview will handle the installation and calibration of SBP, SSS, and MAG systems. Additionally, Geoview will ensure that all equipment calibrations meet Orsted's requirements.

GEOVIEW will take the lead in managing the overall survey operations.

The on-site Surveyor will monitor data quality in real-time, while QA/QC will be conducted at GEOVIEW's office. GEOVIEW will also handle subsequent data processing and interpretation.

Please refer to the EPR for MHCC reporting.

No on-site HSE Manager will be assigned.

comments.comment_text:
'GEOVIEW plans to deploy GEOVIEW DP-1 for Lot 1 & 3 scope, which will be used to conduct MBES, SSS, MAG, SBP and UHRS surveys (UHRS is only included in Lot 1 scope).'
- Please specify the UHRS is only included in Lot 1 scope.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP section 4.1.1

comments.comment_text:
Please provide more details for the Sunyoung No.2 vessel, for examples:
- to specify Sunyoung No.2 is 12hr operation vessel and her plan to run the lines (2 passes, MBES/SBP first pass and SSS/MAG second pass as mentioned in 10.1.2).
- please also add vessel operational/weather limits for Sunyoung No.2.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP section 4.2 and table 10.

comments.comment_text: Thanks for adding Sunyoung No.2 weather limit in Table 10. Regarding weather limit and local requirement (Sunyoung No.2 can only operate in 'coastal area' as comment #23 a), can Geoview advise how much Sunyoung No.2 can support Lot 2 scope where DP-1 cannot do due to fishing gear/water depth constraints?
comments.response_text: Primarily, Sunyoung No.2 will first complete the survey scope assigned to Lot 3. Afterward, we will assess the coverage that can be handled by DP-1 and proceed with further discussions accordingly.

comments.section: 4.3. Management of Guard Vessel Operations, p.20 of 85
comments.comment_text:
'Cross-check weather conditions using reliable sources such as KMA and Windy. Operations will be postponed or canceled if wave heights exceed 1.5 m to ensure the accuracy of scout data, and safety precautions will be taken for conditions above 3 m.'
- will you also use StormGeo for the weather assessment?
- will it be same weather limits for Sunyoung No.2's guard vessel (vessel tbd)?
comments.response_text:
Weather assessments are conducted using KMA, Windy, and StormGeo.
The guard vessel will have different weather limits, which will be updated once the vessel is confirmed.

comments.comment_text:
More details to be provided for Sunyoung No.2 vessel:
- 5.1. Computer Systems: will 5.1 content also the set up (e.g. Figure 8/ Table 9) for Sunyoung No.2 as well? If not, please speficy that only apply to DP-1 and provide details for Sunyoung No.2.
- 5.2. Internet: please speficy the Starlink is for Geoview DP-1. What's the internet/communication set up for Sunyoung No.2, e.g. how vessel can communicate with onshore people when being offshore? Please note that Geoview should also provide internet access to Orsted's Offshore Supervisor onboard.
comments.response_text:
SUNYOUNG No.2 does not have a dedicated internet system onboard.
Since the vessel operates near shore, mobile networks are available, allowing sufficient internet access through tethering.

comments.comment_text: Do you plan to put any spare/back-up equipment onboard Sunyoung No.2? If so, what's the amount and the spec?
comments.response_text: Sunyoung No.2 is a small vessel, so no spare equipment will be placed onboard.

comments.comment_text: If equipment change needed, do you have spares in Geoview/Sunyoung's warehouse (in Busan?) and how much time would it take to send the spare/back-up equipment onboard?
comments.response_text: Spare equipment is stored in the warehouse located in Busan. It takes approximately 5 hours to transport the spare/back-up equipment onboard.

comments.section: 7.2.2. Shipping Lanes and Obstacles within the Survey Area, p.39 of 85
comments.comment_text:
'As shown in Figure 16,, the red box area overlaps with the survey lines of GEOVIEW DP-1 and the ship lane. GEOVIEW DP-1 can only conduct surveys during the daytime under VTS control in this area. Additionally, it cannot survey near the light due to safety distance considerations.'
'This may affect the overall survey schedule, and the survey vessel in use may need to be changed. The survey around the light can be conducted using Sunyoung No.2.'
- what do you mean we cannot survey near the 'light'?
- can you explain the difference between Figure 15 and Figure 16?
- note that we cannot change the vessels since we've submitted the MSRA detailed survey plan to MOF (survey vessels got fixed), and we have confiremd with Geoview no changes to the vessels before the submission.
comments.response_text:
Light means a lighthouse.
Geoview prioritizes safety during survey operations.
DP-1 is a 1,700-ton vessel, making sudden stops and sharp turns difficult while operating equipment. Additionally, it must avoid close approaches to marine objects.
Therefore, Sunyoung No.2, which offers greater maneuverability and is more suitable for operations near marine objects, may be used for surveys in this area.
Figure 14 indicates the overlapping section between the survey area and the ship lane.
The boxed area in Figure 15 shows that the water depth is very shallow, making it inaccessible except during high tide. Therefore, detours are required when passing through this area.
This information has been updated in PEP section 7.2.2.

comments.section: 7.2.3. Tidal Gauge Installation and Retrieval Method, p.40 of 85
comments.comment_text: When will Geoview plan to install the tidal gauge in Lot 2? How long will it take and will that be done during the Geoview DP-1 mob process?
comments.response_text: The tidal gauge will be installed after the Mobilization & Calibration process. The installation is expected to take approximately 2 hours.

comments.comment_text:
'GEOVIEW's dedicated vessel, GEOVIEW DP-1, will undergo UHRS mobilization and calibration at Pyeongtaek Harbor. Since the same equipment used in Lot 2 will be utilized, no additional mobilization or calibration is required.'
- Outdated info, Geoview DP-1 has used Incheon North Port for the ongoing mobilisation/calibration for Lot 1 (UHRS scope).
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP section 8.1

comments.comment_text: Typo for the 8.2.1.2. title 'Sea Acceptance Tests'.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP section 8.2.1.2

comments.comment_text:
'The offset of the SBP will be measured during mobilization and will be installed using a side mount.'
- Can you provide more details of the side mount method, e.g. side pole with how much meter sticking into the water? is the submerged depth of side pole adjustable?
- Any concerns for shallow water and entanglement risk?
comments.response_text: Geoview typically installs the SBP approximately 70 cm below the water surface, but the final installation depth will be determined after testing, considering factors such as vessel roll, pitch, weather conditions, and site environment. Additionally, the submerged depth can be adjusted if necessary, and measures are being considered to mitigate risks related to shallow water operations and entanglement with fishing gear.

comments.comment_text: I understand that the MBES is yet to be installed during my vessel visit on 19th Feb 2025. Please make sure the offset measured after all equipment installed (no movements again), since the parameters will affect the positioning checks in the following mobilisation/calibration process.
comments.response_text: Noted.

comments.section: 10.1.1. Underwater Towed Equipment (GEOVIEW DP-1), p.54-55 of 85
comments.comment_text: Geoview has showed 3 types of different configuration/ tow plan for Lot 1 scope, can you add all 3 types of consideration/suggestion in the PEP? Can Geoview clarify how you plan to run the lines with different UHRS towed method (Figure 23 or Figure 24), i.e. with which sensor on line/ to follow the line plan?
comments.response_text:
Figure 23,24,25 have been updated PEP.
The line plan will be executed based on the GPS installed on the vessel.

comments.section: 10.1.1. Underwater Towed Equipment (GEOVIEW DP-1), p.54-55 of 85
comments.comment_text: Can you also reduce the 96-channel streamer to a 48 & 24-channel streamer if needed? Please mention these options in the PEP.
comments.response_text: It has been updated in PEP 10.1.1.

comments.comment_text: The Sunyoung No.2 has the advantage of being small, able to manourer around fishing gear. Do Geoview see any problems allocating DP-1's work to the Sungyoung No.2, if the DP-1 cannot access certain areas?
comments.response_text: Sunyoung No.2 is only permitted to conduct surveys within coastal areas as specified in its vessel inspection certificate. Surveys cannot be conducted outside the designated coastal area.

comments.comment_text: Thank you for the line plan for all Lots. Please update the name 'Attachment A_Orsted_Incheon_Surveymap_LOT2' on Azure
comments.response_text: It has been uploaded Azure.

comments.comment_text: what sidescan range will you be using? This is the distance from the nadir (each side). We need to ensure we are still able to detect 1m objects.
comments.response_text: The sonar is planned to operate with a range of 100m per side. When operated at a 100m range, the expected resolution is 0.5m(for HF data). If the water depth is shallower than 10-15m, the sonar range may be adjusted to a smaller value.

comments.comment_text: How Geoview plan to deploy the SVP and the measurement frequency for Sunyoung No.2, is 6hrs possible?
comments.response_text: The SVP will be manually deployed from Sunyoung No.2 and measured every 6 hours.

comments.comment_text: The SVP frequency can be adjusted to not to interfere the survey operations (not to stop the lines), which can be carried out as close to required frequency as possible or do them during the line turns.
Please clarify what is a 'MBES cover line'? Are these essentially 'infill lines' in shallow waters?
comments.response_text: MBES Cover Line refers to additional planned survey lines to ensure full MBES coverage in areas identified as shallow water regions.
